results.title: AI/ML Engineer Job in Fort Worth | Lockheed Martin
highlights:
AI/ML Engineer Job in Fort Worth | Lockheed Martin
[...]
Machine-Learning Alert
[...]
# AI/ML Engineer
[...]
$90,000 - 130,000
[...]
Throckmorton Street 1000, Fort Worth
[...]
- We require a bachelors degree in a relevant discipline such as computer science, electrical engineering, mathematics, or a related engineering field. - We are looking for at least 5 years of experience in artificial intelligence or a closely related area. - We need a strong foundation in software engineering, AI, and machine learning. - We value solid expertise in signal processing theory and practical applications, including radar, communications, electronic warfare, sonar, audio, or biomedical domains. - We expect experience with modern AI methods such as computer vision, deep learning, and reinforcement learning. - We prefer proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, or C++. - We prefer hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX, or Keras. - We prefer experience using Python libraries such as NumPy, OpenCV, scikit-learn, pandas, and SciPy. - We look for self-motivation, a strong work ethic, and effective time management. - We value excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
[...]
## Responsibilities
[...]
- We develop and apply AI technologies to address complex signal processing challenges and deliver innovative solutions. - We contribute across the full system development lifecycle, including requirements definition, system and software design, implementation, integration, and flight testing. - We take ownership of critical assignments and lead technical teams to produce high-quality outcomes. - We help craft proposals and white papers to support funding for AI research and development efforts. - We serve in technical lead roles and mentor junior engineers to strengthen their capabilities. - We collaborate with cross-functional partners to build and deploy AI solutions. - We explain complex technical ideas and solution concepts to non-technical stakeholders. - We work with other teams across our organization to identify and advance opportunities for AI adoption.
[...]
We are part of Lockheed Martins Artificial Intelligence Center and our Applied AI team focuses on developing technologies and products that use artificial intelligence to deliver advanced signal processing capabilities to our customers. We offer the opportunity to work on high-impact programs ranging from early-stage research to deployment in a fast-paced, innovative, and collaborative environment. We also support professional growth through mentorship and development opportunities. Preferred work locations are our facilities in Fort Worth, Texas, or Palmdale, California, and remote arrangements may be considered for qualified candidates.
[...]

results.title: Data Engineer III - REMOTE, US | Hiretium Job Board
highlights: At RTX, the world largest aerospace and defense company, 185,000 great minds are united by purpose and inspired to make a difference solving the world’s most complex problems. With our three market leading businesses, world-class operations and investments in research and development, we offer capabilities and opportunity no one else can. Together, we push the boundaries of known science and find new ways to connect and protect our world. Raytheon brings the strength of more than 100 years of experience and renowned engineering expertise to meet the needs of today’s mission and stay ahead of tomorrow’s threat. We deliver solutions that help our nation and allies defend freedoms and deter aggression, creating a safer, more secure world. Join us and help shape the future of aerospace and defense. At Raytheon, the foundation of everything we do is rooted in our values and a higher calling – to help our nation and allies defend freedoms and deter aggression. We bring the strength of more than 100 years of experience and renowned engineering expertise to meet the needs of today’s mission and stay ahead of tomorrow’s threat. Our team solves tough, meaningful problems that create a safer, more secure world. The Business Analytics team in Raytheon Digital Technology Operations, Supply Chain, and Quality is looking for a data engineer focused on the development and deployment of data-driven systems solutions designed to improve performance in the planning and execution of our business. Primary areas of focus will include analyzing source enterprise resource planning (ERP) system information, curating and developing complex datasets that achieve business objectives while meeting data engineering best-practices. Our team collaborates closely with stakeholders and analysts within operations, supply chain, quality, and other core execution functions to identify supply and demand risks and provide recommendations through performant and trusted data products and complex data models. The role offers opportunity to participate in building various business critical solutions as well as the opportunity to solve unique business problems that arise on ad-hoc basis. The ideal candidate needs to be passionate about proper data engineering standards with analytical mindset to drive action through facts and data, possess the ability to effectively organize and distill data, proactively ask appropriate questions to understand hypotheses and requests, and analyze and communicate complex data in a compelling manner to a variety of audiences. The candidate will have the opportunity to build strong partnerships and networks across the organization to learn the Raytheon business and grow as in a fast-paced environment. What You Will Do Develop and optimize complex batch and near-real-time SQL queries to meet product requirements and business needs. Architect, implement, deploy, and maintain data-driven solutions in Snowflake and Databricks. Develop and manage data pipeline supporting multiple reports, tools, and applications. Conduct advanced statistical analysis to yield actionable insights, identify correlations/trends, measure performance, and visualize disparate sources of data. Lead and engage in requirement planning, trainings, and presentations with technical and non-technical teams What You Will Learn SAP ERP/MES data sets, transactions, and backend databases Understanding of manufacturing and supply chain processes Foundational data sources and key performance metrics depended by Raytheon Operations and Supply Chain to build quality data products Designing, troubleshooting, and maintaining tasks, procedures, views, and ETL pipelines Qualifications You Must Have Typically requires a University Degree or equivalent experience and minimum 5 years prior relevant experience, or An Advanced Degree in a related field and minimum 3 years of experience. Strong experience with Snowflake and relational SQL database design, implementation, and

Posted Jun 6, 2026

GenAI Engineer - Associate Data Scientist

### Location: Irving United States

Job Description:

Title: AI/ML / Generative AI Engineer

### Location: Irving, Tx (Hybrid - 3 days onsite)

- Strong Data Structures Any Language Code Comprehension Python or Java

Skills

Other details

The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum salary target for the position across all US locations. results.title: Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
highlights:
Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
[...]
# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
[...]
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date May 15, 2026
[...]
## Job Details
[...]
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
[...]
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
[...]
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
[...]
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
[...]
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
[...]
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
[...]
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
[...]
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
[...]
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
[...]
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
[...]
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems
[...]
Experience building LLM-powered applications, including at least two of: RAG systems, text-to-SQL, agentic workflows, or fine-tuning pipelines
[...]
Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
[...]
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
[...]
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions
[...]
Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies
[...]
Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases
[...]
Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems
[...]
Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems
[...]
$181,500 - $283,800

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
[...]
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:

This role leads the strategic adoption of AI, particularly Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank to enhance productivity and reduce costs. Requires 8+ years in predictive analytics, proficiency in Python or R, and experience with machine learning and statistical modeling.

Lead and execute advanced analytics projects utilizing machine learning, simulation, and optimization techniques to generate actionable insights.

Python R SQL NoSQL Machine Learning Statistical Modeling Regression Decision Trees Clustering Algorithms Support Vector Machines

About The CompanyUSAA is a leading financial services organization dedicated to serving the unique needs of military members and their families. Our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service, and trusted advice. We strive to be the preferred choice within the military community by upholding our core values of honesty, integrity, loyalty, and service. USAA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive environment where our team members can grow and thrive. We also take pride in supporting active-duty military spouses, offering flexible work arrangements such as remote or hybrid options, in line with company policies and business needs. Our organization values innovation and continuous improvement, making us a dynamic and rewarding place to build your career.About The RoleWe are seeking a highly skilled Lead AI Individual Contributor Data Scientist to spearhead the strategic adoption and practical application of artificial intelligence, with a particular focus on Generative AI, within USAA Federal Savings Bank (USAA FSB). This pivotal role involves identifying innovative AI solutions, whether emerging industry technologies, existing tools from other USAA divisions, or integrated platform solutions, to enhance productivity, automate processes, reduce operational costs, and unlock new revenue streams in the financial services sector. The successful candidate will actively scout and evaluate external AI tools and technologies through various channels, including professional networks, academic collaborations, industry conferences, and research publications, ensuring USAA remains at the forefront of technological advancements. The role emphasizes the development of intelligent work assistants that streamline routine tasks, thereby improving the member experience and operational efficiency while considering ethical implications and future potential of AI innovations.This position offers remote work flexibility within the continental U.S., with occasional travel requirements. Candidates residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office are expected to work on-site four days a week. Relocation assistance is available for qualified applicants seeking to join our team.QualificationsThe ideal candidate will possess a strong educational background in a quantitative discipline such as mathematics, computer science, statistics, economics, finance, actuarial sciences, or engineering. A minimum of eight years of experience in predictive analytics or data analysis is required, with advanced degrees (Master’s or PhD) in relevant fields and at least six years of related experience preferred. Candidates should have extensive experience in training, validating, and deploying advanced analytics models, including machine learning, physical models, and statistical techniques.

results.title: Senior AI/Data Engineer - Cotton Holdings Inc.
highlights:
Senior AI/Data Engineer - Cotton Holdings Inc.

## Overview

Location: Houston, TX (Hybrid Preferred) or Remote (U.S./LATAM)Type: Contract-to-HireLevel: Mid-Senior

## About the Role

We’re hiring an AI & Data Platform Engineer to build AI-powered systems that automate RFP discovery, document analysis, and qualification workflows.

You’ll work on backend AI systems that ingest RFPs, extract key information from documents, build semantic search, and help teams decide which opportunities to pursue.

This is a hands-on engineering role focused on production systems — not just prompt engineering.

## What You’ll Do

Build AI workflows for RFP intake, document parsing, extraction, and qualification

Create RAG and semantic search pipelines over RFP documents

Extract structured data like due dates, project scope, contacts, requirements, and submission instructions

Develop FastAPI services and backend APIs

Build human review and approval workflows

Improve reliability, testing, observability, and AI quality

## Tech Stack

Python

FastAPI

Pydantic / Pydantic AI

PostgreSQL + pgvector

SQLAlchemy

Azure OpenAI

Azure Blob Storage

Prefect

Docker

GitHub

## Requirements

4+ years of software engineering experience

2+ years building backend APIs or data platforms in production

Strong Python backend engineering experience

Experience with LLMs, RAG, embeddings, or AI workflow automation

Experience with PostgreSQL and relational databases

Strong understanding of data modeling and validation

Ability to build clean, reliable, maintainable systems

## Nice to Have

LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, or similar frameworks

OCR, PDF parsing, or document intelligence experience

Azure AI services experience

Vector database or pgvector experience

Prefect, Airflow, or orchestration tools

Experience with construction, procurement, legal, or document-heavy workflows

## Ideal Candidate

You’re a pragmatic engineer who understands that production AI requires more than prompts. You know how to build scalable systems with strong validation, clean architecture, and reliable workflows.

Apply for this Job

Tagged as: AI, docker, Git, LLM, machine learning, mid-level, PostgreSQL, Python, RAG, senior, SQL

results.title: Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI) | Jobspawn
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI) | Jobspawn

Jobspawn.← All Jobs

# Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Posted Jun 8, 2026

Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Experience: 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

Employment Type: Full-Time (W2 Only)

### Location: USA (Hybrid / Onsite)

Work Authorization: H4 EAD, L2S, GC EAD, Green Card, US Citizen

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly accomplished Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics, along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

This role requires ownership of GenAI solution design, model development, experimentation, deployment, and performance optimization across enterprise environments.

- Key Responsibilities
- Define and lead GenAI and data science strategy across business use cases.
- Design and implement LLM-based applications including RAG architectures.
- Lead development of machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions.
- Perform deep exploratory data analysis (EDA) and derive actionable insights.
- Architect end-to-end ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring).
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.
- Establish best practices for MLOps, model governance, and evaluation frameworks.
- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.
- Drive innovation in AI/ML and Generative AI solutions.

Required Technical Skills

- Data Science & ML
- Strong expertise in Machine Learning & Statistical Modeling
- Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn)
- Feature engineering, model evaluation, and tuning

- Generative AI
- LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, LLaMA, etc.)
- Prompt engineering and LLM orchestration
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- LangChain / LlamaIndex / Hugging Face

- Data & Processing
- SQL
- Large-scale data handling and transformation

- Cloud & MLOps
- AWS / Azure / GCP
- MLflow / SageMaker / Azure ML
- CI/CD for ML pipelines
- Model deployment and monitoring

- Preferred Qualifications
- Experience with Deep Learning (TensorFlow / PyTorch)
- Exposure to vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma)
- Experience with Databricks or big data platforms
- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills

- Eligibility & Compliance
- W2 Full-Time Only
- No C2C
- No consultancy or vendor profiles

How to Apply

Share your updated resume to:

manish.g@precisiontechcorp.com

Apply for This Job

Build and maintain requisite data solutions , including vector databases, embedding pipelines, and knowledge bases to support retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and document understandingCollaborate with other data scientists to design, code, train, test, deploy, and iterate on machine learning systemsExplore model-driven reasoning through the application of prompt engineering, model fine-tuning techniques (supervised, LoRA,PEFT, etc.), and optimization methods (quantization, distillation, caching) as part of day-to-day development work.Create technical documentation, runbooks, and training materials for model usage and maintenance. Accomplishes objectives with a high degree of self sufficiencyCollaborate with other Data Scientists, as well as engineers from other disciplines to complete daily tasksActively participates in cross-functional teams to develop AI and ML solutionsStay current on GenAI/LLM research, best practices and technology roadmap Achieves business goals, shares learnings, knowledge and skills and promotes cross-functional teamwork.Raise the level of competency in the team by seeking and sharing domain knowledge in the area of Data Science, as well as Novo Nordisk’s therapy areas
[...]
- Master’s degree or PhD highly preferred. Minimum of Bachelor’s degree required. Degree within subject matter expertise preferredMaster’s Degree with 4+ years’ relevant experience, or PhD with 2+ years’ relevant experience, or a Bachelor’s degree with 6+ years’ relevant experience can be consideredRelevant experience includes:Experience working in LLM and generative AI domain: model selection, system design, fine-tuning (supervised, LoRA, PEFT, etc.), prompt engineering, evaluation, observation, etc. A foundational understanding of deep/reinforcement learning models and architectures such as transformers, GANs, VAEs, etc. Experience in designing agentic solutions to tackle problems in healthcare, with considerations on evaluation and harnessing. Proven programming skills in Python and familiarity with data science and ML tooling (e.g., Jupyter Notebook, HuggingFace, PyTorch)Experience building data pipelines and retrieval systems (vector databases,embedding pipelines, knowledge bases) to support RAG, Graphs, and document understanding workflows

# Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Posted May 28, 2026

Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

- Experience:
- 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

- Employment Type:
- Full-Time (W2 Only)

- Location:
- USA (Hybrid / Onsite)

- Work Authorization:
- H4 EAD, L2S, GC EAD, Green Card, US Citizen

Job Summary

- We are seeking a highly accomplished
- Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise
- to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in
- machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics
- , along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

- This role requires ownership of
- GenAI solution design, model development, experimentation, deployment, and performance optimization
- across enterprise environments.

- Key Responsibilities
- Define and lead
- GenAI and data science strategy
- across business use cases.
- Design and implement
- LLM-based applications including RAG architectures
- .
- Lead development of
- machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions
- .
- Perform deep
- exploratory data analysis (EDA)
- and derive actionable insights.
- Architect end-to-end
- ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring)
- .
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.
- Establish best practices for
- MLOps, model governance, and evaluation frameworks
- .
- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.
- Drive innovation in
- AI/ML and Generative AI solutions
- .

- Required Technical Skills
- Data Science & ML
- Strong expertise in
- Machine Learning & Statistical Modeling
- Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn)
- Feature engineering, model evaluation, and tuning

- Generative AI
- LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, LLaMA, etc.)
- Prompt engineering and LLM orchestration
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- LangChain / LlamaIndex / Hugging Face

- Data & Processing
- SQL

- Cloud & MLOps
- AWS / Azure / GCP
- MLflow / SageMaker / Azure ML
- CI/CD for ML pipelines
- Model deployment and monitoring

- Preferred Qualifications
- Experience with Deep Learning (TensorFlow / PyTorch)
- Exposure to vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma)
- Experience with Databricks or big data platforms
- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills

- Eligibility & Compliance

- No C2C
- No consultancy or vendor profiles

manish.g@precisiontechcorp.com
